patch.value: Promote schema, config, profile, and init primitives to the public SDK: full workspace customization programmatically

Typed SDK functions for runtime-schema management (types/statuses/fields/presets), settings read/write with validation, profile list/show/apply/lint, and workspace init/scaffold — completing the universal-customization story. Duplicate check: profile-presets/plan are partially exported already; this item completes and stabilizes the domain surface.

SDK exports typed schema/config/profile/init primitives with the same validation + never-block errors as the CLI; corresponding commands consume only these primitives; docstrings + linked tests + SDK reference docs; zero behavior change

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation slicing 2026-07-10 (grounded in current src state). Already public — do NOT redo: profile read-side (presets/plan/describe/lint exported via src/sdk/index.ts), schema runtime registries (resolveRuntimeFieldRegistry/resolveRuntimeStatusRegistry via src/sdk/runtime.ts), and generic PmClient.runTyped wrappers for the schema/config/profile/init actions. The actual gap: domain logic lives in CLI command modules — src/cli/commands/schema.ts (1973 lines), config.ts (1576), init.ts (955) + init-agent-guidance.ts (566), ~5.1k lines total — so SDK consumers reach these domains only through generic action dispatch, not typed primitives. Suggested slices: S1 schema mutation primitives (~150m) — extract add-type/add-field/status-def write paths into src/core/schema (fields-file/item-types-file/status-defs-file already own the file formats); command becomes parse+render. S2 config/settings primitives (~90m) — extract read/write/validate onto src/core/config/nested-settings; preserve the settings validator's unknown-key-stripping semantics (PR#314). S3 init + workspace-discovery primitive (~90m) — single source of truth for discovery; this is where the GH-501/pm-69nl class (init target invisible to discovery) gets fixed at the primitive level — coordinate with pm-69nl, do not duplicate its fix. S4 typed PmClient facades + SDK reference docs/tests (~60m) — replace generic payloads with typed inputs/outputs; each primitive must match the equivalent runAction result shape (semantics oracle, pm-usfg 07-06 note). Order: S1 first (schema gates the pm-w89s universality story); S2/S3 independent; S4 last.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Final branch evidence: schema inspection/mutation ownership moved from the CLI into src/sdk/schema.ts; the CLI is now a compatibility re-export and SDK runtime/MCP consume the SDK-owned engine. The import-boundary ratchet removed 17 schema edges, stale suppressions were deleted, and the hard suppression budget fell 174 -> 170. Exact verification on the final source: 290 files / 5,701 tests; 36,848/36,848 statements, 26,463/26,463 branches, 7,438/7,438 functions, 35,963/35,963 lines; typecheck, strict lint, zero-clone, 100% module/export/member docstrings, package boundary, token budget, docs, contracts, security, version, packed npm smoke, 88-command dogfood, and fresh external PmClient schema/custom-type lifecycle all pass. Broader config/profile/init implementation ownership remains for this feature, so it returns to open.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Moving a CLI command engine into the SDK must also migrate its lint baseline, residual test mocks, and runtime imports; keeping a tiny CLI re-export preserves compatibility while the SDK becomes the single implementation owner.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #565 round-1 hardening and acceptance evidence: accepted actionable Greptile/CodeRabbit findings for scoped history validation, strict global author parsing, exact config integer validation, atomic guidance writes, pre-mutation init validation, resolved-workspace guidance, and fixture fidelity. Promoted the extension lifecycle engine into SDK ownership when review exposed the remaining init-to-CLI boundary. Final exact all-source gate: 291 files / 5,713 tests; 36,940/36,940 statements, 26,521/26,521 branches, 7,450/7,450 functions, 36,055/36,055 lines. Strict lint, 100% module/export/member docstrings, package boundary, token budget, zero clones, contracts, and static ratchets pass. Fresh packed-tarball temp consumer installed all 12 aliases, initialized a real tracker with persistent author_default, created/commented with invocation overrides, validated zero history drift/unknown authors, managed 11 unique extensions, and imported the SDK-owned CLI program/scanner/extension engine successfully.

value.text: External analyzer convergence evidence 2026-07-15: DeepScan identified one unused legacy config-key array and one logically unreachable item-format conflict (the only valid format is toon). Removed both dead paths and the two artificial tests that supplied an impossible internal json_markdown value; real policy and criteria conflicts remain covered. Pruned two now-stale ESLint suppressions, ratcheted the suppression ceiling to the exact current 165 and the coverage-pragma ceiling to the exact current 477, and explicitly configured DeepSource for the repository's ES-module system rather than excluding analyzer scope. Focused config tests pass 86/86; strict lint, zero-clone, 100% docstring, package-boundary, and token gates pass. Final all-source proof passes 291 files / 5,719 tests at exact 100%: 36,976/36,976 statements, 26,535/26,535 branches, 7,458/7,458 functions, 36,091/36,091 lines.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: DeepSource follow-up correction: its PR run still parsed changed .mjs gates as classic scripts because repository .deepsource.toml changes are activated only from the default branch; this created one parser error plus 1,937 cascading false alerts. The ESM configuration fix remains for post-merge/future runs. To keep this PR analyzable without weakening mandatory policy, restored the gate module itself byte-for-byte to main and moved the exact 165 ESLint-suppression / 477 coverage-pragma ceilings into the mandatory package quality:static invocation. pnpm quality:static passes with those exact limits. This supersedes only the earlier statement that the defaults inside static-quality-gate.mjs were ratcheted; the effective mandatory gate remains exact.

The repository's own stronger 100% docstring/static/complexity gates remain green.
